title: CUBE formula zero-result debugging across MDX member, measure grain, and cell formatting
originSignal: A CUBEVALUE cell returns zero or an unexpected blank even though the user expects a non-zero value.
riskPattern: The issue can be in the MDX member path, measure numerator/denominator grain, filter clearing logic, or worksheet display format.
regressionChecks: Extract CUBE formulas and referenced measures from the workbook report layer.
Trace measure names through escaped MDX references.
Separate report-layer reference errors from Data Model measure behavior.
Record display-format checks when a small numeric result might be hidden.
expectedEvidence: CUBE dependency report lists formulas, measures, members, and parsing diagnostics.
Measure impact report identifies direct and helper-cell downstream references.
Task notes isolate whether the issue is formula reference, model grain, or display formatting.
boundaries: OpenXML inspection sees formula text but cannot evaluate CUBEVALUE results.
Live model evaluation requires Excel desktop with the workbook Data Model available.
nextLiveWorkbookRequirement: Add a sanitized workbook with CUBE formulas that include direct measure references, helper cells, and one intentionally wrong member path.
